p-hertz.caption: Hertz's spark oscillator and resonant loop (1887)
p-hertz.spoken: Hertz's spark-gap oscillator exciting a resonant loop detector

p-emwave.caption: A radiated electromagnetic wave: E ⊥ H ⊥ direction of travel
p-emwave.spoken: an electromagnetic wave showing the electric and magnetic fields at right angles to each other and to the direction of propagation

p-flambda.caption: Wavelength vs frequency across the amateur bands (λ = 300 / f_MHz)
p-flambda.spoken: a curve of wavelength against frequency, with the amateur bands marked

spark-tx.caption: A spark-gap transmitter: the induction-coil source charges the primary tank (C1) through the telegraph key; when the spark gap fires, C1 rings down through the primary (L1) of the oscillation transformer, inducing a damped oscillation in the tuned secondary (L2 and variable C2) that feeds the antenna and ground.
spark-tx.spoken: a spark-gap transmitter with the induction coil, key, spark gap, tuned tank, and antenna

damped-vs-cw.caption: A damped spark wave-train versus a pure continuous wave: the spark transmitter (top) produces repeated bursts of exponentially decaying oscillation, each burst's energy dumped and gone before the next spark fires; a continuous-wave (CW) transmitter (bottom) sustains a constant-amplitude sine carrier for as long as it is keyed.
damped-vs-cw.spoken: two waveforms: an exponentially decaying spark train above, a steady continuous wave below

crystal-det.caption: A crystal (cat's-whisker) detector receiver: antenna and ground drive a parallel LC tuned circuit; a point-contact crystal detector (a fine cat's-whisker wire touching a galena crystal, drawn here as a diode) rectifies the tuned RF, and the resulting audio is heard in headphones, with a bypass condenser (Cb) shunting residual RF around them.
crystal-det.spoken: a crystal set: antenna, tuned circuit, galena cat's-whisker detector, and headphones

inverted-l.caption: An inverted-L antenna worked against ground: a vertical wire rises from the station's feed point to a long horizontal top wire, insulated from its two support masts, with a separate earth-ground rod completing the return path -- the ancestor of the ground-plane monopole.
inverted-l.spoken: an inverted-L antenna: a vertical rise, a long horizontal top wire, and an earth ground

regen-rx.caption: A regenerative detector with tickler feedback: amplified plate current from the tickler coil (L2) couples back into the grid tank coil (L1), boosting gain and selectivity.
regen-rx.spoken: Figure 2.1 shows a regenerative receiver where a tickler coil feeds amplified signal back to raise gain and selectivity.

hartley.caption: A Hartley oscillator: a single tapped inductor and its tuning capacitor form the tank, with the centre tap grounded to the cathode so the plate-side and grid-side turns feed energy back into oscillation.
hartley.spoken: Figure 2.2 shows a Hartley oscillator with its tapped tank inductor setting the frequency.

skip-zone.caption: Ionospheric skip and the skip zone: a sky wave refracts off the ionosphere to land far beyond the transmitter's short-range ground wave, leaving a dead zone of no reception in between.
skip-zone.spoken: Figure 2.3 shows a short-wave signal refracting off the ionosphere to a distant landing point, leaving a skip zone of no reception.

mopa.caption: A master-oscillator power-amplifier (MOPA) transmitter: a stable low-power oscillator drives a buffer and then a power amplifier into the antenna, so the antenna's shifting load never pulls the oscillator off frequency.
mopa.spoken: Figure 2.4 shows a MOPA chain: a stable low-power oscillator driving a power amplifier into the antenna.

superhet-block.caption: The superheterodyne receiver: f_IF = |f_RF − f_LO|
superhet-block.spoken: a superheterodyne block diagram: RF amp, mixer with local oscillator, IF amplifier, detector, and audio

pierce-osc.caption: A Pierce crystal oscillator
pierce-osc.spoken: a Pierce oscillator using a quartz crystal as the frequency-controlling element

am-spectrum.caption: The spectrum of a plate-modulated AM signal
am-spectrum.spoken: an AM spectrum: a carrier line flanked by upper and lower sidebands

pentode.caption: The elements of a pentode vacuum tube
pentode.spoken: a pentode tube cross-section: cathode, control grid, screen grid, suppressor grid, and plate

pi-network.caption: A pi-network output tank matches the PA plate to the antenna: a shunt tune capacitor, series inductor, and shunt load capacitor transform impedance while filtering harmonics.
pi-network.spoken: a pi-network output tank: shunt input capacitor, series inductor, shunt output capacitor

yagi.caption: A three-element Yagi–Uda beam: reflector, driven element, and director mounted on a boom, with the main lobe directed toward the director.
yagi.spoken: a Yagi beam: reflector, driven element, and director on a boom, with the main lobe toward the director

yagi-pattern.caption: The azimuth radiation pattern of a Yagi beam, showing a strong forward lobe and much smaller side and back lobes.
yagi-pattern.spoken: a polar radiation pattern with a large forward lobe and small back lobe

classc.caption: Class-C amplifier operation: biased well beyond cutoff, the plate current flows only in short pulses, under 180° per cycle, at the peaks of the grid drive.
classc.spoken: class-C operation: the plate current flows only in short pulses at the peaks of the drive

ssb-filter.caption: SSB by the filter method
ssb-filter.spoken: the filter method: a balanced modulator makes double sideband, then a sharp filter removes one sideband

ssb-phasing.caption: SSB by the phasing method
ssb-phasing.spoken: the phasing method: two balanced modulators fed audio and carrier in 90-degree quadrature cancel one sideband

ssb-vs-am.caption: Occupied spectrum: AM versus SSB
ssb-vs-am.spoken: AM with carrier and two sidebands versus SSB with one sideband and no carrier, showing SSB's narrower, more efficient spectrum

ce-amp.caption: An NPN common-emitter amplifier
ce-amp.spoken: a common-emitter transistor amplifier with base bias, collector load, and emitter resistor

fm-detector.caption: An FM discriminator (frequency detector)
fm-detector.spoken: an FM detector that converts frequency deviation into an audio voltage

repeater-duplex.caption: FM repeater duplex operation with an offset
repeater-duplex.spoken: a repeater receiving on one frequency and simultaneously transmitting on an offset frequency through a duplexer

doppler.caption: Doppler shift across a satellite pass
doppler.spoken: the received frequency sweeping high-to-low through the nominal frequency as the satellite passes overhead

transponder.caption: A satellite linear transponder
transponder.spoken: a satellite transponder receiving an uplink band and re-transmitting it, inverted, on a downlink band

pll.caption: A phase-locked-loop (PLL) frequency synthesizer. A crystal reference and a divided-down VCO output are compared in a phase detector; the error signal is smoothed by a loop filter and steers the VCO. The programmable ÷N divider sets the channel: f_VCO = N·f_ref.
pll.spoken: a PLL synthesizer: reference, phase detector, loop filter, VCO, and programmable divider

ax25-frame.caption: The AX.25 packet frame: HDLC-style Flag, Destination and Source addresses (each with SSID), optional digipeater addresses, Control, PID, Information, and a 16-bit CRC-CCITT Frame Check Sequence, closed by a final Flag.
ax25-frame.spoken: the fields of an AX.25 frame: flag, addresses, control, PID, information, frame check, flag

afsk.caption: Bell 202 1200-baud AFSK: the audio tone shifts continuously between the 1200 Hz mark and 2200 Hz space frequencies as each data bit is sent.
afsk.spoken: a frequency-shift-keyed audio waveform switching between the mark and space tones for ones and zeros

packet-net.caption: A packet network: two stations out of direct radio range each link to a central digipeater, which relays AX.25 frames hop-by-hop between them.
packet-net.spoken: two stations out of direct range relaying through a digipeater node

sdr-iq.caption: A software-defined radio: I/Q direct-conversion front end.
sdr-iq.spoken: a direct-conversion receiver splitting the signal into in-phase and quadrature channels sampled and processed in software

waterfall.caption: An FFT waterfall (spectrogram) of a busy band.
waterfall.spoken: a waterfall display with frequency across and time down, signals appearing as bright vertical traces

psk31.caption: PSK31: BPSK phase reversals carrying Varicode.
psk31.spoken: a binary phase-shift-keyed carrier reversing phase to send varicode characters

ft8-seq.caption: An FT8 exchange in 15-second time slots.
ft8-seq.spoken: an FT8 QSO proceeding in alternating fifteen-second transmit and receive slots

modes-timeline.caption: A century of the art: key technologies by decade
modes-timeline.spoken: a timeline of amateur radio technologies from spark to software-defined radio across the decades
